title: Dense Smoky Chinese Rice Fried Rice
description: A nostalgic, smoky fried rice with toasted butter, tofu, and bell pepper, made Jain-friendly with no eggs or root vegetables.

ingredients.name: butter
ingredients.notes: to be toasted for smoky flavor

instructions: Heat a large wok over medium heat and melt the butter until it turns golden brown and fragrant.
Add the diced bell pepper to the wok and stir-fry for 2 minutes or until slightly softened.
Toss in the cubed tofu and cook until the edges are golden and the tofu is heated through, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the cooked rice, ensuring that the grains are well coated with butter and oil.
Pour the soy sauce and sesame oil over the rice, mixing thoroughly so the sauce is evenly distributed.
Fold in the frozen peas, cooking for an additional 2 minutes until the peas are bright green and heated.
Season the fried rice with a pinch of salt if desired, then remove from heat.
Serve hot, garnished with sliced green onions, and enjoy the dense, smoky flavor.
